message:
perf(btree): relax #[inline(always)] to #[inline] on cursor hot paths + fold payload fast path

Two small btree cursor cleanups left over from the earlier
"split with_btree_op hot path from tracing" commit (1ec9206c).

crates/fsqlite-btree/src/cursor.rs

1. with_btree_op / finish_btree_op_postlude: downgrade
   #[inline(always)] to #[inline].  The earlier split already pulled
   the tracing/telemetry work out of the fast path, so forcing
   always-inline here just bloated code size at every call site
   without measurably improving hot-path latency.  #[inline] still
   lets LLVM inline at its own discretion when it's beneficial.

2. BtCursor::insert_rowid_fast_path (payload mutation fast path):
   collapse the nested `if ... { if let Some(_) = mutate_payload_result? { ... } }`
   into a single expression `if ... && mutate_payload_result?.is_some() { ... }`.
   Identical semantics, one less indent level, and clippy's
   collapsible_if lint no longer fires on it.

No behavior change, no test updates needed — the payload fast path
is covered by the existing btree INSERT/UPSERT suite.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
